states.name: Mirai
states.title: Sparkling Idol
states.description:
Even as the world crumbles, nothing draws a crowd quite as big as Mirai's.  

It only makes sense that people line up in droves to see a Vessel in person. Never mind the ugly business of the Requiem. Mirai's voice is that of the Goddess, and it's said that all who hear it are blessed.  

Indeed, reports of civil disturbance plummet in the wake of her concerts. Everyone seems just a little happier. Nothing actually changes for the people, of course. A happy song can't end a famine or turn back a flood. But it can make them forget for a time. 

It's her duty to make them forget.

states.name: Mirai
states.title: Sparkling Idol
states.description:
It's not that Mirai doesn't like being an idol. In fact, singing is the greatest joy she has in life. She sang long before she was put on stage. She thought, in her early days at the academy, that if she worked hard enough, they would let her leave and travel the world. 

She thought correctly. At ten years old, the Church began training her for the demanding lifestyle of an idol. She didn't mind the rigor, nor the invasion of her privacy. After all, her life was never really hers anyway. 

Sometimes she wishes she could sing as herself, without using the Goddess's voice. Just once, to see if the people still love her. 

She likes to think they would.

states.name: Mirai
states.title: Sparkling Idol
states.description:
A voice is an instrument. It puts thoughts into words, nothing more. It does not think for itself, and certainly doesn't have its own will. 

That was her first lesson as an idol. She came in with a juvenile scrapbook of song ideas, only to be told she would never sing them. The ones she had written for her were made by true artists, she was told. She couldn't expect to ever match them. 

Mirai understands, in a way. But she can't stop scribbling down poems, or plucking out the melodies she hears on the wind. They might be the work of an amateur, but they are hers. 

And she hopes one of them can live on once she's gone.
